reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal statement. The hypothesis `[Group G] [Fintype G]` with `card G = 351` correctly encodes "group of order 351". The conclusion `∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal` asserts existence of a prime `p` dividing `|G|` together with a Sylow p-subgroup `P` that is normal, which matches "has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order" exactly. In Mathlib, `Sylow p G` is defined as a maximal p-subgroup and is always nonempty, so the `p.Prime` conjunct is needed and correctly captures the "prime" requirement. The quantifier structure (∃ prime p, ∃ normal Sylow p-subgroup) matches the informal claim precisely.
